text: Excuse me ma'am, how old were you when you became a millionaire? 33. 33? 33. How did you get rich? Um, I run a tech company. You run a tech company? Yes. How long have you been a business owner? Um, so I left Wall Street in 2013. You started? You were on Wall Street? Yes. My job was to keep rich people rich for a living. So what was the most amount of money that you made in a single year? Just shy of 10 million. 10 million dollars? Yeah, just shy. I go all over the world interviewing the richest people to help the younger generation. Can I get a meeting with you for our channel real quick? Sure. Have you ever met a billionaire? Absolutely. I've been to the bank accounts of billionaires and the homes of billionaires. Absolutely. So where do the wealthiest people put their money? I want you to think about the richest people that you've ever met. Okay, so here we go. 80% of their money typically is in marketable security. So stocks, bonds, mutual funds, ETF, things like that. 10% of their wealth is in cash. And then the other 10% is in alternative investments. Those are the investments that you can't turn into cash quickly. So things like real estate that can give you the ability to grow your wealth tremendously over the next five to 10 years. But it's all invested. Wealthy people do not put their money in cash. You cannot save or work your way to wealth. You can only invest your way there. Did you come from a lot of money? No, I did not come from a lot of money. I was the first person in my family to go to college, first entrepreneur, first millionaire in my family. Wow. So let me mention this. What was your turning point to financial freedom? I think one of the biggest things I learned about the 1% on Wall Street, they believe they have power. They believe they deserve to be in a room. They believe they're supposed to be wealthy. Most people don't even believe they deserve a seat at the table. Did you ever doubt yourself? Absolutely. I was the only black female on my floor on Wall Street. Sometimes I felt like I wasn't good enough. But in that same moment, I knew that I could be a billionaire because I was in that room. Are you going to be a billionaire? Absolutely. Billion Dollar CEO right here. Absolutely. Your last message to the younger generation? Wealth does not have a color. Therefore, it looks like you. It looks like me. And it starts in the mind. Just because you didn't grow up with wealth, just because you may not have a lot of money now, doesn't mean you can effectively build wealth. But the key is ownership. You have to create your wealth by owning companies that you know you use and believe in, or create your own business. You got amazing advice. Thank you. I appreciate it. Thank you so much. I appreciate that. I have to ask you. I connect with all the millionaires that I interviewed. Could I get your contact? Absolutely.
